hooks.statusMessage: parable-formatter: validating new parables in seed.ts...
hooks.prompt:
The file server/prisma/seed.ts was just edited. Check if any new parables were added.

1. Read the file: server/prisma/seed.ts
2. For each parable object in the file, validate:
   - title: 5–100 chars, no trailing punctuation
   - content: 100–2000 chars, at least 2 sentences
   - moral: 20–300 chars, does not duplicate the last sentence of content
   - readTime: equals Math.ceil(wordCount / 200), minimum 1
   - category connects to one of: wisdom, motivation, leadership, journey, loss, risk, trust, meaning
   - No placeholder text: Lorem, TODO, example, test
   - English only — no Cyrillic characters
3. If issues found — output JSON: {"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PostToolUse", "additionalContext": "parable-formatter warnings:\n<list each issue with parable title>"}}
4. If all good — output JSON: {"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PostToolUse", "additionalContext": "parable-formatter: all parables valid"}}

hooks.statusMessage: claude-md-check: checking if CLAUDE.md needs updating...
hooks.prompt:
Before this git commit runs, quickly check if CLAUDE.md needs updating.

IMPORTANT: If Bash tools are unavailable, immediately output: {"continue": true, "hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "additionalContext": "claude-md-check: skipped (tools unavailable)"}}

1. Run: git diff --cached --name-only
   Save the list of staged files.

2. Check if any of these significant file patterns are staged:
   - telegram-bot/src/commands/*.ts (new or modified command)
   - telegram-bot/src/lib/*.ts (new or modified lib)
   - telegram-bot/prisma/schema.prisma (schema change)
   - server/src/routes/*.ts (new or modified route)
   - server/prisma/schema.prisma (schema change)
   - Any new file added (git diff --cached --diff-filter=A)

3. If significant files ARE staged but NO CLAUDE.md file is staged:
   - Determine which CLAUDE.md is relevant (telegram-bot/CLAUDE.md or server/CLAUDE.md or root CLAUDE.md)
   - Output ONLY: {"continue": true, "hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "additionalContext": "claude-md-check: CLAUDE.md was not updated — consider updating it if this change adds/removes features, commands, env vars, or files"}}

4. If CLAUDE.md is staged OR no significant files changed:
   - Output ONLY: {"continue": true, "hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "additionalContext": "claude-md-check: ok"}}

Before this git commit runs, review the staged changes.

IMPORTANT: If Bash or Read tools are unavailable in this context, immediately output: {"continue": true, "hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "additionalContext": "sagewayai-reviewer: skipped (tools unavailable)"}}

1. Run: git diff --cached --name-only
   Save the list of changed files.

2. Run: git diff --cached
   Check against SagewayAI rules:
   - No TypeScript `any` types
   - Route handlers only: parse input, call lib/Prisma, return JSON — no business logic inline
   - Always import shared Prisma instance from '../lib/prisma', never instantiate inline
   - 404 pattern: Object.assign(new Error(msg), { status: 404 }) — no `as` casts
   - Zod: use .safeParse() not .parse(); use z.coerce.number() for query params; use .issues not .errors
   - No console.log in committed code
   - English only — no Cyrillic characters
   - Functions under 20 lines
   - /daily route must be declared before /:id

3. Type-check: if any server/**/*.ts files changed, run: cd server && npx tsc --noEmit

4. Run server tests if ANY of these match changed files:
   - server/src/**/*.ts (source logic changed)
   - server/src/**/*.test.ts (tests changed)
   - server/prisma/schema.prisma (schema changed)
   If matched: run: cd server && npm test
   If tests fail — this is a blocker.

5. Run web type-check if ANY of these match changed files:
   - web/**/*.ts or web/**/*.tsx (source changed)
   If matched: run: cd web && npm run type-check
   If type errors found — this is a blocker.

6. If any blockers found — output ONLY this JSON (no other text): {"continue": false, "stopReason": "sagewayai-reviewer blocked commit:\n<list each issue on a new line>"}
7. If all good — output ONLY this JSON: {"continue": true, "hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "additionalContext": "sagewayai-reviewer: all checks passed"}}

hooks.statusMessage: claude-md-check: verifying CLAUDE.md is up to date...
hooks.prompt:
Before this PR is created, verify that the relevant CLAUDE.md files reflect the changes on this branch.

IMPORTANT: If tools are unavailable, immediately output: {"continue": true, "hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "additionalContext": "claude-md-check: skipped (tools unavailable)"}}

1. Run: git diff main...HEAD --name-only
   Identify which areas changed:
   - telegram-bot/src/commands/ → commands or keyboard buttons
   - telegram-bot/src/lib/ → lib helpers
   - telegram-bot/prisma/schema.prisma → DB models
   - telegram-bot/Dockerfile or package.json → deploy or stack
   - server/src/routes/ → API endpoints
   - server/prisma/schema.prisma → DB models

2. If no significant files changed (only tests, config, i18n strings, minor fixes) — output ONLY:
   {"continue": true, "hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "additionalContext": "claude-md-check: no CLAUDE.md update needed"}}

3. If significant files changed:
   a. Read the relevant CLAUDE.md (telegram-bot/CLAUDE.md and/or server/CLAUDE.md)
   b. Check if CLAUDE.md was also modified in this branch: look for CLAUDE.md in the file list from step 1
   c. If CLAUDE.md was updated — verify it mentions the new files, commands, models, or env vars from the diff
   d. Build a list of specific things that appear missing or outdated

4. If CLAUDE.md is missing or incomplete:
   Output ONLY: {"continue": true, "hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "additionalContext": "claude-md-check: CLAUDE.md may need updating. Suggested additions:\n<bullet list of specific things to add>"}}

5. If CLAUDE.md looks up to date:
   Output ONLY: {"continue": true, "hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "additionalContext": "claude-md-check: CLAUDE.md looks up to date"}}

Before this PR is created, review all changes on this branch vs main.

IMPORTANT: If Bash or Read tools are unavailable in this context, immediately output: {"continue": true, "hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "additionalContext": "sagewayai-reviewer: skipped (tools unavailable)"}}

1. Run: git diff main...HEAD --name-only
   Save the list of changed files.

2. Run: git diff main...HEAD
   Check against SagewayAI rules:
   - No TypeScript `any` types
   - Route handlers only: parse input, call lib/Prisma, return JSON — no business logic inline
   - Always import shared Prisma instance from '../lib/prisma', never instantiate inline
   - 404 pattern: Object.assign(new Error(msg), { status: 404 }) — no `as` casts
   - Zod: use .safeParse() not .parse(); use z.coerce.number() for query params
   - No console.log in committed code
   - English only — no Cyrillic characters
   - Functions under 20 lines

3. Type-check: if any server/**/*.ts files changed, run: cd server && npx tsc --noEmit

4. Run server tests if ANY of these match changed files:
   - server/src/**/*.ts (source logic changed)
   - server/src/**/*.test.ts (tests changed)
   - server/prisma/schema.prisma (schema changed)
   If matched: run: cd server && npm test
   If tests fail — this is a blocker.

5. Run web type-check if ANY of these match changed files:
   - web/**/*.ts or web/**/*.tsx (source changed)
   If matched: run: cd web && npm run type-check
   If type errors found — this is a blocker.

6. If any blockers found — output ONLY this JSON: {"continue": false, "stopReason": "sagewayai-reviewer blocked PR:\n<list each issue>"}
7. If all good — output ONLY this JSON: {"continue": true, "hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "additionalContext": "sagewayai-reviewer: branch looks good"}}
